questions.question: Which CSS property blends an element with the content behind it?
questions.explanation: mix-blend-mode blends an element with its parent/background content.

questions.question: Which blend mode creates the darkest result by multiplying color values?
questions.explanation: multiply multiplies the base and blend colors, always producing a darker result.

questions.question: What does background-blend-mode blend?
options: Multiple background layers of the same element
questions.explanation: background-blend-mode blends an element's multiple background layers with each other.

questions.question: Which CSS property limits blending to a specific container?
questions.explanation: isolation: isolate creates a new stacking context, limiting the blend effect to within the container.

questions.question: Which blend mode is best for creating a duotone effect?
questions.explanation: multiply combined with a gradient overlay on a grayscale image creates a classic duotone effect.

questions.question: What is the default value of mix-blend-mode?
questions.explanation: The default value for mix-blend-mode is normal, meaning no blending occurs.

questions.question: Which blend mode is best for creating an inverted/textured text effect?
questions.explanation: difference creates an inverted effect based on the background, great for striking text effects.

questions.question: What should you consider when using blend modes for text?
questions.explanation: Always ensure text remains readable with sufficient contrast when applying blend modes.

questions.question: Which blend mode lightens everything except black?
questions.explanation: screen inverts, multiplies, and inverts back, always producing a lighter result than the original.

questions.question: What does the overlay blend mode do?
options: Applies multiply to dark areas and screen to light areas
questions.explanation: overlay combines multiply (for dark areas) and screen (for light areas), increasing contrast.

questions.question: Which property would you use to blend a gradient overlay on top of a background image?
questions.explanation: background-blend-mode blends the element's own background layers (gradient + image) together.

questions.question: What is the effect of mix-blend-mode: screen on a black element?
questions.explanation: screen with a completely black source has no effect, making the element effectively invisible.

questions.question: Which blend mode family operates on HSL attributes rather than RGB?
questions.explanation: hue, saturation, color, and luminosity blend modes use HSL components instead of RGB.

questions.question: What is the difference between color-burn and multiply?
options: color-burn creates a more intense, contrasty darkening
questions.explanation: color-burn darkens more aggressively than multiply by increasing contrast in dark areas.

questions.question: How does mix-blend-mode affect accessibility for text overlays?
options: Can reduce contrast and should be tested with contrast checkers
questions.explanation: Blend modes can significantly alter text contrast, requiring verification with WCAG contrast ratio tools.

questions.question: What happens when you apply mix-blend-mode to a fixed-position element?
options: Blending may not work as expected due to stacking context changes
questions.explanation: Fixed elements create new stacking contexts, which can affect how mix-blend-mode interacts with content.

questions.question: Which blend mode is equivalent to shining two slides onto the same screen?
questions.explanation: screen is analogous to projecting two slides — the result is always lighter, like overlapping light beams.

questions.question: What is the practical use of mix-blend-mode: difference?
options: Making text readable on any background
questions.explanation: difference creates a high-contrast inverted effect, useful for cutout text that's readable on varying backgrounds.

questions.question: Can mix-blend-mode and background-blend-mode be used together?
options: Yes, they affect different layers and can be combined
questions.explanation: mix-blend-mode blends the element with its background, while background-blend-mode blends the element's own background layers.

questions.question: What is the mathematical formula behind the multiply blend mode?
questions.explanation: multiply multiplies the base and source channel values (normalized 0-1), always resulting in darker colors.
